highlights: Vietnam's largest monolithic granite rock at 200m long, resembling a giant elephant
Panoramic views of Central Highlands farmland, ethnic villages, and Chu Yang Sin mountain range
Short 15-20 minute climb to summit with peaceful atmosphere ideal for photography

thingsToKnow.crowds: Not heavily crowded; quieter during off-peak times and national holidays
thingsToKnow.difficulty: Steep granite surface requires careful footing; slippery when wet. Challenging descent. Suitable for children with supervision.
thingsToKnow.safety: Hold hands with young children on ascent/descent due to uneven surfaces and height exposure. Very windy at summit, requires caution for kids.
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Manageable for children as young as 3-5 years old with assistance, but not wheelchair accessible
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Best visited morning or evening to avoid intense midday heat. Not recommended on rainy days due to slippery rock surface

visitorTips: Bring sports shoes or sneakers for better grip on smooth granite surface
Visit during golden hour for optimal photography and cooler temperatures
Claim your included homemade drink ticket after purchase to refresh after climb

faq.question: Is there an entrance fee?
faq.answer: Yes, typically 50-60k VND per person, which includes one complimentary homemade drink. Children under 5 often enter free. Some local sites may request voluntary tips instead.

faq.question: Can young children climb this rock?
faq.answer: Yes, children ages 3-5 can reach the top with adult supervision and hand-holding. The ascent is manageable but steep; descent is more challenging. Keep toddlers close due to height exposure.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: Early morning or evening for photography and cooler temperatures. Avoid 10am-3pm intense heat and rainy days when the granite becomes slippery and dangerous.

sentiment.positive: Visitors praise the geological uniqueness, manageable climb, peaceful summit atmosphere, cultural significance, reasonable pricing, included refreshments, and family-friendliness despite physical challenge
sentiment.negative: Some criticize commercialization through entrance fees and restricted access that developed over time; concerns about artificial infrastructure additions to natural site
